methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 18 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vast, a late-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Jake Cannell and Christian Horne, has raised over $300M and carries a current post-money valuation of $2.0B. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 18 same-sector comparable companies spanning both public and private stages, including public comps (Planet Labs at $7.0B, Firefly Aerospace at $6.3B) and private peers (Relativity Space at $4.2B, Astranis at $3.5B, ICEYE at $2.8B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Late Stage advancement, yields $2.4B. Peer-median analysis across 18 Space & Aerospace comparables at similar valuation tiers implies $1.4B, with the peer set ranging from $400M to $7.0B. Public-comp anchoring via Price-to-Sales multiples (median 64.2x across 6 tickers including RKLB and PL) is not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue data for the private target.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $1.2B (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $2.0B valuation sits at the upper-median of this range. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue visibility for direct revenue multiple comparison and the wide dispersion across public peers, which reflects heterogeneity in business models and development stage within the aerospace sector.
